summary: Recent updates in the Firefox browser's media decoding capabilities focus on improving video processing and compatibility across different platforms. Notably, there is enhanced **support for H.264 video encoding**, which includes stricter checks on picture dimensions to avoid errors, and ensures that only valid dimensions are utilized. The adjustments also introduce **AImageReader support for Android**, allowing better handling of images and video frames within applications. This is critical for ensuring smooth and high-quality playback of video content, particularly on mobile devices. Overall, these improvements aim to enhance the browser's performance and reliability when handling various media formats, benefiting users with a better viewing experience.
